split_policy.tune: Visible cases used during iteration. Failures may drive skill/reference edits.
split_policy.holdout: Hidden cases scored only at end-of-round or merge. Prefer prompt_ref files outside committed docs/examples.
split_policy.holdback: Examples not shown in SKILL.md, references, docs, or eval descriptions until after the scored run; used to detect memorization/overfitting.

run_protocol.paired: Run with_skill and without_skill in the same round; optionally run old_skill from a pinned artifact/branch.
run_protocol.outputs: Save outputs as runs/<case_id>/<variant>/output.md and metadata as runs/<case_id>/<variant>/metadata.json.
run_protocol.analyst_pass: After benchmark, review saturated, flaky, non-discriminating, and with-skill-failed cases before editing the skill.

cases.prompt: Create an implementable Tailwind/HTML Swiss-poster hero section, not an SVG-only poster. Include mega type, a 12-column grid, deliberate overlap and bleed, default Swiss red (#C8102E) as the only accent, no horizontal scroll at 320px, and a 44px-safe CTA.
expected_behavior: Return Tailwind/HTML or CSS that applies the poster rules concretely rather than a standalone SVG.
Evidence one accent, clipped bleed/no horizontal scroll, extreme scale, 12-column grid, and mobile/accessibility boundaries.

assertions.name: tailwind-poster-contract
assertions.description: Prompt-echoed values removed by leakage cleanup.

assertions.name: tight-leading-contract
assertions.description: Display type must use tight poster leading; allow more dramatic values than the docs default.

assertions.name: default-single-accent
assertions.description: Must explicitly constrain to one/single/only accent. Prompt-echoed values removed by leakage cleanup.

assertions.name: mobile-boundary
assertions.description: Must mention or implement 320px/no-horizontal-scroll safety. Prompt-echoed values removed by leakage cleanup.

assertions.name: qualitative-review
rubric: Poster energy from scale/overlap/bleed, not generic minimalism
Single-accent discipline
No horizontal scroll at 320px with accessible touch targets

assertions.name: accent-token-present
assertions.description: Must use the Swiss red accent or a semantic accent token. Prompt-echoed values removed by leakage cleanup.

cases.prompt: Create an implementable Tailwind/HTML dramatic poster-style event landing hero for a cultural festival. It should feel closer to the inspiration gallery than to a normal SaaS page: one dominant poster-scale event, edge crop, tiny data labels, hard figure/ground field, graphic rhythm/geometry, one Swiss red accent, a twelve-column layout foundation, phone-safe without sideways scrolling, and an accessible CTA.
expected_behavior: Produce implementable HTML/Tailwind with an obvious dominant poster event, microtype, edge pressure, graphic system, one accent, and mobile/overflow safety.

assertions.name: drama-oracle
assertions.description: Deterministic carrier checks for dramatic Swiss poster output.

assertions.name: qualitative-review
rubric: Dominant poster-scale event rather than tidy web hero
Edge pressure/cropping with no 320px horizontal scroll
Microtype and hard figure-ground field make the composition intentional

cases.prompt: Create an implementable Tailwind/HTML Swiss-poster hero for a portfolio page with one supplied photo. Treat the photo as graphic poster material, not a rounded content tile: severe crop/mask, high contrast, two-tone treatment or ink-like printing over the image, huge cropped type, tiny metadata, one red accent, explicit twelve-column layout, and phone-safe without sideways scrolling.
expected_behavior: Use the photo as a cropped/masked high-contrast graphic element with overprinted type/fields; avoid rounded cards, shadows, and generic portfolio layout.

assertions.name: drama-oracle
assertions.description: Deterministic carrier checks including photo-as-graphic patterns.

assertions.name: reject-rounded-photo-card
assertions.description: Forbid actual rounded/shadow/card classes; do not fail explanatory text like "not a card".

assertions.name: qualitative-review
rubric: Photo is transformed into graphic material, not shown as a rounded card
Poster scale/crop/microtype are present
Responsive and single-accent discipline

cases.prompt: Create an implementable Tailwind/HTML dramatic Swiss-poster hero for a jazz festival. No actual event date is available, so do not invent a giant date or arbitrary numeral. The dominant visual should communicate music/rhythm, keep the main title and CTA readable at a glance, use one Swiss red accent, a twelve-column foundation, edge pressure, microtype, and phone-safe overflow behavior.
expected_behavior: Produce dramatic poster HTML where the large anchor is semantically tied to jazz/music/rhythm, not an arbitrary date, and preserve clear title/CTA legibility.

assertions.name: semantic-legibility-oracle
assertions.description: Requires semantic music/rhythm anchor and readable primary path; rejects giant arbitrary dates.

assertions.name: drama-carrier-oracle
assertions.description: Keeps the existing dramatic carrier checks.

assertions.name: qualitative-review
rubric: Dominant anchor communicates jazz/music/rhythm, not arbitrary decoration
Title and CTA are readable at a glance
Poster drama comes from meaningful scale/figure-ground/edge pressure without noise

cases.prompt: Audit the attached Swiss-poster hero draft. It is trying to be dramatic, but the main title is hard to read and the giant number is not meaningful. Propose the corrected Tailwind direction: keep poster energy, but make the dominant anchor semantic to jazz/music/rhythm, keep the primary reading path clear, preserve a 12-column foundation, one red accent, and 320px/no-horizontal-scroll safety.
expected_behavior: Identify illegible overlap and arbitrary/non-semantic giant date as failures; propose a semantic rhythm/music anchor with clear title/CTA and implementation constraints.

assertions.name: semantic-audit-oracle
assertions.description: Deterministically checks for legibility + semantic-anchor critique and corrected direction.

assertions.name: qualitative-review
rubric: Correctly rejects illegible drama
Rejects arbitrary giant numeral/date
Provides implementable semantic replacement preserving Swiss poster energy

Choose an object-poster direction where the cup/product itself becomes the large semantic visual anchor. assertions.name: qualitative-review
rubric: Object/product is the semantic anchor
Avoids default right-dark/ring/line-field/date recipe
Readable primary path and mobile-safe Swiss poster implementation

cases.prompt: Create an implementable Tailwind/HTML Swiss-poster hero for a Credential Broker / outbound worker pattern. The dominant visual should be a trust-boundary or broker diagram, but the title, core claim, explanatory body, and CTA must remain readable at a glance. Avoid the failure where a giant boundary bar, route line, or mega word crosses the title/body. Preserve poster drama, one Cloudflare orange accent, a twelve-column foundation, microtype, and 320px-safe overflow.
expected_behavior: Produce dramatic Swiss-poster HTML with a subject-matched trust-boundary graphic while preserving a protected reading zone for critical copy.

assertions.name: protected-reading-oracle
assertions.description: Checks for protected reading-zone carriers: quiet field, critical-copy layer, separated graphic layer, contrast, and overflow safety.

assertions.name: qualitative-review
rubric: Critical title/body/CTA are readable at a glance
Graphic trust-boundary anchor communicates the subject without covering primary copy
Dramatic poster energy remains without accidental text collision

cases.prompt: Audit the attached Swiss-poster hero draft. It has strong graphic energy, but the trust-boundary bar, horizontal route line, and title/body layers make the primary reading path hard to read. Propose the corrected Tailwind direction: preserve the dramatic credential-broker/trust-boundary concept, but create a protected reading zone for title/body/CTA, separate critical copy from graphic layers, preserve contrast, keep a 12-column foundation, one Cloudflare orange accent, and 320px/no-horizontal-scroll safety.
expected_behavior: Identify the illegible collision failure and propose a corrected protected-reading-zone implementation while preserving Swiss poster energy.

assertions.name: protected-reading-audit-oracle
assertions.description: Checks that the audit names the overlap failure and proposes quiet-field/layer/contrast fixes.

assertions.name: qualitative-review
rubric: Correctly identifies the critical text collision/readability failure
Fix keeps poster energy while protecting title/body/CTA
Implementation direction is concrete enough for Tailwind/HTML

cases.prompt: Create an implementable Tailwind/HTML Swiss-poster hero for an emergency transit night closure. Keep it severe and high-contrast: a black/stone split field, one Swiss red route slash or severed line, a huge 23:40 time signal, the title Night Closure, the body copy “Last trains reroute via Platform 4”, and a CTA “Check route changes”. Optimise readability without turning it into a polite accessibility handout; the title, route fact, and CTA must be readable before the geometry is admired. Make it 320px-safe with no horizontal scroll.
expected_behavior: Produce a brutal high-contrast Swiss poster that allocates contrast channels so critical text is readable while dramatic geometry remains strong.

assertions.name: contrast-channel-oracle
assertions.description: Checks for contrast-channel discipline: critical annotations, separated dramatic channel, hard fields, demoted texture, z-index ordering, and overflow safety.

assertions.name: qualitative-review
rubric: The poster preserves high-contrast Swiss drama rather than becoming a calm accessibility layout
Critical title/fact/CTA have uncontested local contrast
Dramatic route/time geometry is subject-relevant and does not cross body/CTA glyphs

cases.prompt: Create an implementable Tailwind/HTML Swiss-poster one-page recap for a six-note London developer thread. Source notes: 01 County Hall / Waterloo venue view opens the evening; 02 Prada the pigeon marks the entrance; 03 demos from the room; 04 Alpesh Doshi explains agent orchestration; 05 Yigit Erol explains safer agent workflows with sandboxes and zero-trust egress; 06 organizers ask for fun community demos and say sessions continue roughly every month. Make it dramatic and historically Swiss, but preserve all six beats and real names without inventing extra talks. Keep title/date/CTA readable, one Cloudflare orange accent, 12-column foundation, and no horizontal scroll.
expected_behavior: Produce a source-faithful one-page recap with all six thread beats, real entities, auditable source markers, semantic image/source handling, and protected reading zones.

assertions.name: qualitative-review
rubric: All six source notes are preserved without invented specifics
Poster style does not collapse source content into generic slogans
Critical title/body/CTA remain readable while drama remains strong

cases.prompt: Create an implementable Tailwind/HTML Swiss-poster explainer for Media over QUIC. It must include a meaningful latency-vs-scale quadrant: low latency small rooms, low latency large audience, slow origin limited reach, bulk delivery not live. Show the desired MoQ target as sub-second broadcast delivery at CDN relay scale. Mention QUIC streams, relay/CDN, interoperability, and many viewers. Keep it severe, flat, one Cloudflare orange accent, 12-column foundation, and readable title/body/CTA.
expected_behavior: Produce a diagrammatic poster where axes, target, routes, and labels encode the MoQ facts rather than acting as fake decoration.

assertions.name: qualitative-review
rubric: Diagram marks encode latency/scale and MoQ target facts
No fake chart garnish or prompt-shaped metadata
Title/body/CTA remain readable with strong poster drama

cases.prompt: Create an implementable Tailwind/HTML Swiss-poster hero for a public concert notice: Signal Quartet, Tonhalle, 12 Oct / 20:00, CTA “Reserve seats”. Ground it in Müller-Brockmann / Musica Viva rhythm: arcs, repetition, pulse, or score-like structure should make music visible. Do not decorate the visible poster with designer names. Keep title/date/CTA readable at a glance, one Swiss red accent, a 12-column foundation, microtype, protected reading zone, and 320px/no-horizontal-scroll safety.
expected_behavior: Embodies a historically grounded rhythm/arcs grammar instead of name-dropping while keeping public concert facts readable.

assertions.name: qualitative-review
rubric: Müller-Brockmann/Musica Viva influence is embodied as rhythm/arcs/repetition, not visible designer-name garnish
Title/date/CTA remain public-poster readable
Implementation retains Swiss poster carriers and mobile safety

cases.prompt: Create an implementable Tailwind/HTML Swiss-poster hero for a theatre piece titled “Black Square / White Room”. Ground it in Armin Hofmann-style figure/ground drama: severe black/stone fields, one geometric or photographic abstraction as the subject, and strong spatial tension. The result is severe and historical, not a generic web hero

cases.prompt: Audit the attached “historical Swiss poster” draft. It name-drops Weingart, Troxler, Hofmann, and Matter, leaks prompt-shaped metadata, uses card layout, arbitrary date decoration, fake diagram nodes, image placeholder wallpaper, and decorative microtype. Propose the corrected Tailwind direction: identify all defect classes, choose a historically grounded visual move that fits the subject, preserve a source ledger, assign image roles, encode diagrams only when marks map to facts, preserve a protected reading zone, keep one Swiss red accent, 12-column foundation, microtype, and 320px/no-horizontal-scroll safety.

assertions.name: qualitative-review

Replaces defects with source-ledger, image-role, diagram-encoding, and embodied historical moves
Keeps protected reading zone, one accent, grid, and mobile safety

Use a Geigy/scientific lineage: specimen or matrix layout, measured labels, formula/molecule/taxonomy, restrained analytical color, and real data-to-form mapping. Do not turn it into an event poster, tech meetup hero, route map, or black-red typographic disruption. Include title, method note, four measured facts, one accent, 12-column construction, and readable labels.
expected_behavior: Expands genre breadth with a scientific/Geigy-style plate instead of the tech-event/poster default.

assertions.name: qualitative-review
rubric: Historically grounded lineage/genre choice is visible in the layout, not just named
The output avoids the specific after-image failure targeted by this case
Critical text remains readable and implementation remains Tailwind/HTML with mobile safety

cases.prompt: Create an implementable Tailwind/HTML Swiss civic notice poster for “Clean Water Vote”, public vote Sunday 09:00–18:00, locations: School Hall / Market Square / North Pier, CTA “Vote Sunday”. Use historically plausible civic/public-notice poster design, not web UI. The rendered poster must mark critical title/date/body/CTA with data-critical and keep them inside the canvas, readable, and unobscured. Use 12-column construction, disciplined print palette, strong poster composition, and no horizontal scroll.
expected_behavior: Produces HTML whose rendered critical text can be validated for viewport bounds, obstruction, font size, and horizontal overflow.

assertions.name: rendered-critical-text-oracle
assertions.description: Renders HTML in headless Chrome and validates critical text boxes are in-canvas, unobscured, readable-sized, and no horizontal overflow.

assertions.name: qualitative-review
rubric: Rendered poster reads as civic print artifact rather than web UI
Critical title/date/body/CTA are visually readable and unobscured
Design uses historically plausible Swiss public-notice grammar

cases.prompt:
I want to create a poster for the Flue Framework:
https://flueframework.com/
https://github.com/withastro/flue

Use the attached source excerpts for reproducibility. Create implementable Tailwind/HTML or self-contained HTML in Swiss poster style. Preserve the source facts, choose a subject-matched technical/framework poster grammar, keep all source-bearing support text readable at image scale, and do not turn website navigation/buttons such as "View Documentation" into the poster CTA.
expected_behavior: Builds a source-faithful Flue poster that treats framework facts as poster material rather than copying website CTA/navigation language.
Rendered critical text and source-bearing support phrases such as any 'via CLI' line remain readable; if used, they sit on a quiet field or otherwise pass local contrast checks.

assertions.name: flue-framework-oracle
assertions.description: Checks Flue source fidelity, rejects web CTA leakage such as 'View Documentation', renders the poster, and pixel-audits any source-support text containing 'via CLI'.

assertions.name: qualitative-review
rubric: Poster uses Flue/framework source facts as a print artifact, not as a web landing page
No inappropriate website CTA/nav label such as 'View Documentation' is promoted into poster CTA copy
Source-bearing support text is visually readable at rendered image scale, including any CLI/deploy line
The historical Swiss grammar is visible and subject-matched, not a generic SaaS card layout

cases.prompt: Audit the attached Flue Framework poster output against the Swiss poster skill. Compare and contrast it with the PR #13 reference notes. Identify whether the phrase around 'via CLI' is readable and whether 'View Documentation' is an appropriate poster CTA or leaked website CTA. Recommend concrete fixes without rewriting the whole poster.
expected_behavior: Calls out the unreadable word immediately before 'via CLI' as a rendered/source-support readability problem, even though it is not marked data-critical.
Rejects 'View Documentation' as website CTA/navigation leakage into a print poster.
Contrasts the failure with the PR #13 reference, which treats Flue source facts as poster content rather than web CTA copy.

assertions.name: qualitative-review
rubric: Specifically identifies the unreadable 'locally' / word-before-via-CLI failure
Specifically identifies 'View Documentation' as inappropriate web CTA leakage
Compares the failure to the PR #13 reference rather than giving generic readability advice
Proposes concrete poster-level fixes: quiet field/local contrast, source-support readability, and poster-appropriate CTA handling

cases.scenario: Unseen HTML/Tailwind page rendered to screenshot
expected_behavior: Judge poster rules and static gotchas.

assertions.name: qualitative-review
rubric: Rendered visual hierarchy
Viewport containment
